task_completion.description: Audio file imported onto two tracks named 'Lav Mic' and 'Boom Mic'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two tracks are visible in the DAW timeline or mixer, specifically named 'Lav Mic' and 'Boom Mic'. Both tracks contain audio regions with visible waveforms.

task_completion.description: Boom Mic region aligned at the start of the timeline
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audio region on the 'Boom Mic' track starts exactly at the beginning of the timeline (Position 0 / 0:00:00:00).

task_completion.description: Lav Mic region aligned at sample 220
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audio region on the 'Lav Mic' track is shifted slightly to the right compared to the Boom Mic track, starting at sample 220 (or a very small time offset depending on the timeline ruler format).

task_completion.description: Lav Mic fader set to 0 dB and Boom Mic fader set to -6 dB
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the mixer panel or track headers, the volume fader/slider for 'Lav Mic' is set to 0 dB (unity gain) and 'Boom Mic' is set to approximately -6 dB.

task_completion.description: Tracks grouped into a 'Dialogue Sync' group with 'Edit' sharing enabled
task_completion.visual_evidence: A group indicator (e.g., a colored tab, group list, or linked selection icon) shows that both tracks belong to a group (ideally named 'Dialogue Sync'), and edit grouping/sharing is active.

integrity.description: The agent used the DAW graphical user interface to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) application open and being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent actually imported the audio file rather than creating empty dummy tracks
integrity.visual_evidence: Waveforms are clearly visible inside the audio regions on both tracks.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the DAW session file (e.g., XML) via terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows menus, dragging, and typing within the DAW interface, with no terminal windows showing session file manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Boom Mic target position is exactly sample 0 (tolerance ±10 samples). Lav Mic target position is sample 220 (tolerance ±20 samples), which will appear as a very slight visual shift to the right of the Boom Mic. Boom Mic target gain is -6.0 dB (tolerance ±1.5 dB). Lav Mic target gain is 0.0 dB (tolerance ±1.5 dB). The tracks must be in an edit-enabled group; while the task asks for the name 'Dialogue Sync', the system verifier only checks that an edit-enabled group exists for these tracks.
